action.reasoning: Observation: iteration 7 (CLIP 20 epochs with mixup, label smoothing) achieved Quickdraw 19.5%, down from 21.6% (iteration 6, 5 epochs). Training curves show source-domain overfitting (val acc 81.2%, sketch acc 77.6%) while OOD performance dropped. Analysis: mixup and label smoothing are not helping DG; longer training overfits source domains. Need stronger OOD regularization and better checkpoint selection. Decision: train CLIP with SWAD (stochastic weight averaging densly) which finds flat minima known to improve DG; add heavy sketch‑mimicking augmentations (RandAugment, grayscale, solarize, color jitter) to bridge domain gap. Use 10 epochs, lr=1e‑5, no mixup/smoothing, SWAD start epoch 5. Submit eval immediately to check improvement.

command_output.stdout: SWA model saved (averaged over 6 epochs). Best sketch acc (non-averaged): 0.7716
